description: During the 2016 Australian federal election, the Labor Party launched the 'Mediscare' campaign, claiming the Liberal-National Coalition planned to privatize Medicare. The campaign became a defining factor of the election, turning a perceived government advantage into a close race.

key_events.description: Labor Party sends text messages to voters claiming Medicare is under threat.
Coalition government issues public denials of any privatization plans.
Polling shows significant voter trust shift toward Labor on healthcare issues.
Election result confirms a narrow win for the Coalition with a swing against them.

calibration_notes: Useful for understanding the impact of 'fear-based' political messaging on long-standing public health institutions.
